verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 is an intracellular calcium-release channel resident on the endoplasmic reticulum membrane. It is a multi-pass transmembrane protein (6 TM domains) with its ion-conducting pore and regulatory domains facing the ER lumen and cytoplasm, not the plasma membrane. The protein mediates IP3-stimulated Ca2+ release into the cytosol from ER stores—a purely intracellular signaling function. No documented cell-state-induced ecto-translocation, no tissue-restricted surface display, no lysosomal exocytosis (not a lysosomal resident), no dual PM/ER localization with a functional surface pool, and no covalent surface-partner tethering. The protein lacks any stable physical link to the plasma membrane or its anchored partners. ER-resident intracellular calcium channels do not reach the outer leaflet.
